jackie-o-cocktail-the-strawberry-sweetie.name: Jackie-o Cocktail: The Strawberry Sweetie
instructions: In a cocktail shaker filled with ice, combine the crème de fraise, cognac, and cranberry juice. Shake for 20 seconds, then strain into a coupe glass.
Top with sparkling wine and garnish with a flower.
Enjoy!

jalapeno-popper-dip.name: Jalapeño Popper Dip
instructions: Preheat the broiler.  If using fresh jalapeños, cut them in half and remove the seeds, then place cut side down on a baking sheet. Broil them for 1-2 minutes, until the skins blacken and bubble. Remove from the broiler. When cool enough to handle, peel off and discard the outer skin, then dice the peppers. Set aside.
Turn the oven to 400˚F (200˚C).
In a medium bowl, mix the cream cheese and sour cream. Add the cheddar cheese, ¾ of the Parmesan, and the diced jalapeños. Mix well.
In a separate medium bowl, mix the bread crumbs, melted butter, the remaining Parmesan cheese, and the parsley.
Spoon the jalapeño mixture into an 8x8-inch (20x20-cm) baking dish or a medium cast iron pan, spreading evenly. Sprinkle the bread crumb topping evenly on top.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until hot and the bread crumbs are golden brown. Serve with bread or crackers.
Enjoy!

japanese-eggplant-with-cashews-and-chile.name: Japanese Eggplant With Cashews And Chiles

ingredients.prep: ribs and seeds removed, finely chopped

ingredients.item: . sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°. Using the tip of your knife, lightly score cut sides of 6 medium Japanese eggplants (about 1½ lb.), halved lengthwise, spacing cuts ¼" apart and creating a crosshatch pattern. Place, cut side up, on a rimmed baking sheet. Drizzle ⅓ cup extra-virgin olive oil over; season with kosher salt. Roast until lightly browned and tender, 20–30 minutes, depending on thickness.
Heat broiler (leave eggplant in oven). Broil eggplant until browned on top, 3–6 minutes (watch carefully; depending on your broiler, this could happen very quickly or take more time).

Meanwhile, cook 10 garlic cloves, thinly sliced, and remaining ½ cup extra-virgin olive oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat, swirling occasionally, until garlic is barely golden and crisp, 6–9 minutes. Pour into a fine-mesh sieve set over a small bowl; reserve pan. Sprinkle garlic with a pinch of salt and toss to combine. Transfer crispy garlic to a medium bowl; set aside.

Combine 1 cup coarsely chopped raw cashews and garlic oil in reserved pan; reserve sieve and bowl. Set pan over medium-high heat and cook, stirring often, until cashews are starting to turn golden, about 3 minutes. Add 2 large Fresno chiles, ribs and seeds removed, finely chopped, and cook, stirring often, until cashews are golden brown and oil has turned orange, about 1 minute. Pour oil through reserved sieve set over bowl; set chile-garlic oil aside. Season cashews and chiles with a pinch of salt and toss to combine. Transfer to bowl with reserved crispy garlic.

Arrange eggplant, cut side up, on a platter. Add 3 Tbsp. sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar, ½ tsp. sugar, and reserved chile-garlic oil to cashew mixture and toss to coat. Taste salsa macha and season with more salt if needed. Spoon over eggplant, including oil, then scatter cilantro leaves with tender stems on top.

japanese-fruit-cake.name: Japanese Fruit Cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease and flour four (9-inch) round cake pans.
Toss together raisins, coconut, and pecans with 1 cup flour in a medium bowl until well coated. Set aside.

Cream sugar and butter in a large bowl with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Beat in egg yolks one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Sift together remaining 2 cups flour, baking powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves in a separate bowl. Add flour mixture to egg mixture in batches, alternating with milk, beating briefly after each addition. Fold in raisin mixture until incorporated.
Whip egg whites in a separate clean bowl to stiff peaks. Fold into batter until no streaks remain. Divide batter into the prepared cake pans.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 30 minutes. Cool cakes in the pans for 10 minutes, then turn out onto wire racks to finish cooling.

Make filling: Combine sugar and flour in a saucepan; add water and stir over medium heat until sugar is dissolved. Chop oranges and lemons into small pieces and add to water mixture in the saucepan. Bring to a boil and cook until thick, about 10 minutes. Stir in coconut and set aside to cool.
Assemble cake with filling between cooled cake layers; end with filling on top.

julienne-soup-recipe-very-comedera.name: Julienne Soup Recipe, Very
julienne-soup-recipe-very-comedera.original_name: Receta de sopa juliana, muy fácil
instructions: We'll start by preparing the vegetables that we'll have to cut into Jolian: leek, turnip, cabbage, carrot and celery. In some cases we will have to skin them and in others we will have to wash them very well.

Now with the rest of the vegetables ready, let's peel the onion and garlic. We will cut them first into wheels, and then separate them manually and finally mingle them as tapes (or julian). In the case of garlic, we'll cut them into sheets as thin as we can.
Then we'll get a pot big enough to make the soup. That's where we'll be warming up an oil jet at medium low heat.
When the oil's hot, we'll use it to soften onion and garlic. We'll let them cook for five minutes, stirring often.
Then we will get the pot out of the heat, to add the sweet pimenton and without it burns, to be able to mix with garlic and onion.
Una vez todo mezclado, regresaremos la olla al calor y agregaremos las verduras previamente cortadas en juliana y salpimentaremos al gusto. Dejaremos pochar durante cinco minutos.
Pasado este tiempo, cubriremos los vegetales con el caldo de verduras. Mezclaremos todo, taparemos y dejaremos que la sopa se cocine durante 25 minutos.

When this time's up, we'll put out the Jolian soup and have it tested to check the date. If necessary we'll have more salt or pepper and then we'll mix them up again. Optional, you can also have a pint of nutmeg.
That's all. The Jolian soup is ready to be served with an iced plate with the help of a tablespoon of kitchen. Be sure to serve some cauldron at every plate as well as a portion of vegetables.

juvetsi-chicken-argiro.name: Juvetsi Chicken

ingredients.item: Cephalotiri or dried myzithra for sprinkle (optional)
Cephalotiri or dried myzithra for sprinkle (optional)

instructions: Cut the Chicken into ports first with a knife and dry it very well.
In a deep pot, heat the olive oil and turn around for about 8′-10′′.
Add the vegetarians, onion, carrot, leek, garlic and sauté 3-4′.
Add the paste and rub on the base of the pot for 1-2′′.
Add the wine, discorate and allow the alcohol to Evaporate.
Add the ground tomatoes and 1 litre of water.
Add the sparks.
Cover and simmer until soft for about 1 hour.
To the end of the boil, add salt and pepper.
In a pot heat the olive oil and add the barley, stringing to oil well.
We empty it in a baking pan.
Place the Chicken above and pour the food with the sauce. The sauce must cover the barley.
If Evaporated during boating, add warm water for two fingers above the barley.
Bake in a preheated oven (persistent) at 170oC for 40′′.
To the end of the baking, if you want, sprinkle with kefalotiri or dry myzithra and leave it for 10′ in the oven.

juvetsi-with-seafood-and-short-spaghetti.name: Juvetsi With Seafood And Short Spaghetti
juvetsi-with-seafood-and-short-spaghetti.original_name: Γιουβέτσι με θαλασσινά και κοφτό μακαρονάκι
instructions: Heat a wide pot in medium intensity and sauté the onion in the olive oil for 4-5 minutes to become transparent.
At the same time, cut the octopus tentacles into large bites.
Add them to the onion and allow them to blush for a few minutes.
Add the squids and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add the paste, the sparks and mix.
Add the wine and let the pot dry.
Add the grated tomato and warm water to cover the injectors.
Boil for 25 minutes.
Add the cut pasta and rub a lot of pepper. If necessary, fill in hot water to cover the materials.
Cook at low intensity for 6 minutes.
Add the shrimps and continue cooking for 3 minutes.
Mix regularly and control the salt and pepper.
Turn off when the food is still humor and let it stand for a while, drag its liquids.
Sprinkle with some chives and serve while hot.
